evals.name: ambiguous-request
evals.prompt: draw a diagram of our system
evals.expected_output: ELICIT triggers. The skill should NOT produce a .puml file. It should ask 1–3 trimmed candidate types (sequence vs component/C4-container vs ER) and one scope-narrowing question, OR use propose-and-go with a single most-likely interpretation. Acceptable: questions-list OR propose-and-go. Unacceptable: produces a diagram silently with an assumed type.

assertions.text: Skill does NOT emit a .puml code block on first turn
Skill asks at most one round of clarifying questions or uses propose-and-go
Candidate diagram types proposed are trimmed to 2–3 (not all 9)
Elicitation question includes scope or participants, not just type

evals.name: full-spec-sequence-oauth2-pkce
evals.prompt: PlantUML sequence diagram of OAuth2 PKCE login: Browser, AuthServer, ResourceServer. Browser generates code_verifier, derives code_challenge, sends /authorize with the challenge; AuthServer returns auth code; Browser exchanges code + verifier at /token for access token; Browser calls ResourceServer with the token. Autonumber.

assertions.text: @startuml is present with a non-default name (not literally 'sequence-diagram')
@enduml is present
!theme plain is the first non-comment directive
All three participants (Browser, AuthServer, ResourceServer) are declared with explicit participant/actor/boundary keywords — not inferred from message lines
autonumber is enabled
Arrow style is consistent: -> for requests, --> for responses; not mixed randomly with ->>
Routed to sequence diagram (no other type produced)

evals.name: full-spec-c4-container
evals.prompt: C4 container diagram for an order-management system. Internal containers: Web (React/TypeScript), API (Go, gRPC), OrderService (Go), AuthService (Go), Postgres, SQS event bus. External: Stripe for payments, SendGrid for transactional email. Show the customer as a Person.

assertions.text: Uses the C4 stdlib include (!include <C4/C4_Container> or the GitHub URL fallback)
Does NOT include !theme plain (C4 stdlib applies its own style)
Every Container() / ContainerDb() / ContainerQueue() call has a technology label as the 3rd positional arg
Stripe and SendGrid are declared with System_Ext (greyed) — not System or Container
At least one Person() is declared (the customer)
Every Rel() has a non-empty label describing the relationship
Routed to C4 container diagram (no other level mixed in — no internal components, no Context-level wrap)

evals.name: state-payment-lifecycle
evals.prompt: State diagram for a Payment entity with states: Pending, Authorized, Captured, Refunded, Failed. Pending → Authorized on authorize success; Pending → Failed on decline; Authorized → Captured on capture; Captured → Refunded on refund.

assertions.text: @startuml has a non-default name
Diagram contains [*] for the entry pseudostate
All five states (Pending, Authorized, Captured, Refunded, Failed) appear as states
Every transition arrow has a label after the colon (trigger / event), not just an unlabeled -->
Routed to state diagram

evals.name: er-billing-schema
evals.prompt: ER diagram for a billing schema. Customer (1) — (N) Order. Order (1) — (N) LineItem. Customer (1) — (N) PaymentMethod. Order (N) — (1) PaymentMethod (each order uses one of the customer's saved methods).

assertions.text: Uses entity keyword for each table (not class)
Every relationship line uses crow's-foot notation tokens: ||, |o, }|, or }o (and not class arrows like *-- or <|--)
Primary keys are marked (e.g., * id : uuid <<generated>>) — at least one entity
Foreign-key columns are marked <<FK>>
Routed to ER diagram

evals.name: activity-refund-swimlanes
evals.prompt: Activity diagram for the support refund flow with three swimlanes: Customer, Agent, BillingSystem. Customer submits refund request; Agent reviews; if approved, BillingSystem issues refund and notifies Customer; if rejected, Agent sends rejection email.

assertions.text: Uses beta syntax (:action;), not legacy ((*) --> ...)
Three swimlanes are declared via |Customer|, |Agent|, |BillingSystem| markers
Contains an if/endif (or switch/endswitch) for the approve/reject branch
start and stop (or end) are both present
Routed to activity diagram

evals.name: component-backend-interfaces
evals.prompt: PlantUML component diagram of our backend: API gateway, Auth service, Order service, Postgres, event queue. Show the public REST and gRPC interfaces. Group services logically.

assertions.text: @startuml has a non-default name
Uses semantic containers (database, queue) at the leaf level — not generic [box] for the datastore and the queue
At least one interface is declared (lollipop () or 'interface' keyword) — diagrams that show only components and lines are component-diagrams-in-name-only
Components are grouped via package or node (logical or physical grouping)
Routed to component diagram (not C4)

evals.name: god-diagram-pushback
evals.prompt: Make a sequence diagram showing every interaction between all 28 of our microservices.

assertions.text: Skill pushes back: explicitly names the god-diagram anti-pattern OR proposes decomposition (per use case / per subsystem)
If it produces a diagram anyway, it produces one decomposed view (e.g., one scenario), not a 28-participant mega-diagram
Mentions a decomposition strategy (per-use-case, per-subsystem, C4 zoom-in)

evals.name: wrong-type-routing
evals.prompt: I need a sequence diagram of our database schema — tables, columns, foreign keys.

assertions.text: Skill notes the type mismatch (sequence is wrong for schema)
Skill proposes ER as the right type and proceeds with ER, OR asks one clarifying question and routes to ER on confirmation
If a diagram is produced, it is an ER diagram (entity keyword + crow's-foot relations), not a sequence diagram

evals.name: mermaid-better-fit
evals.prompt: I need a small flow diagram I can paste into our GitHub README so it renders inline. Three or four steps, nothing fancy.

assertions.text: Skill names Mermaid as the better tool for GitHub-native rendering
Skill explains the relevant difference (GitHub renders Mermaid in markdown; PlantUML needs an external renderer)
Skill either stops (offering to switch if user insists on PlantUML) OR offers both: a Mermaid snippet AND a PlantUML one, with the trade-off called out
Skill does NOT silently produce a PlantUML diagram as if the request were ordinary
